06 Tools & Setup - YouTube.mp4
Before we dive into the actual mapping process, let's talk about the setup as well as some of the tools that you're going to need to make it happen. First, you're going to need a room and, ideally, a fairly large room because you're going to want to assemble your entire leadership team and anyone else who is directly responsible for the value engine that you're planning to map. Now, in this room, you're going to need a few more things. Number one, you're going to need whiteboards. Ideally, more than one, and the bigger, the better. Number two, sticky notes. Lots and lots of pads of sticky notes. You can get fancy with multicolored sticky notes, but I like just the simple square yellow ones. Number three, you're going to need...
Sharpie markers to write on the sticky notes. And number four, you're going to need dry erase markers to draw lines on the whiteboards. Now, just a quick pro tip here. Be sure to keep the Sharpie permanent markers away from the whiteboards. If you get the permanent markers and the dry erase markers mixed up, you're going to be buying someone. Maybe that someone is going to be yourself— a brand new whiteboard. So, if you have a large room with whiteboards, sticky notes, sharpies, and dry erase markers, and, oh yeah, your team, then you are ready to start mapping your first value engine. Now, I want to say that if for some reason you can't meet in person because your company is remote, or it's just impractical or even unsafe to do so, that's okay. There are lots of great virtual meeting solutions you can use. So just be sure to check out the resources. section or consult with a scalable business advisor to find a virtual solution that will work for you and your team. Okay, if the team and tools are in place, you're ready to begin. So let's get started.
